info.name: luke-auth-engine
info.description:
Consumer-facing auth gateway for the Luke platform.

It authenticates the caller via a **WorkOS** access token, resolves the WorkOS subject to an engine userId (`workos:<sub>` style), mints a short-lived signed *act-as-user* token (RS256), and transparently proxies the request to `luke-core-engine` (and reads `luke-capability-engine` for the `/session` view).

## Flow
```
consumer-ui --WorkOS access token--> luke-auth-engine --act-as-user JWT--> luke-core-engine
```

## Getting started
1. Set the `baseUrl` collection variable (default `http://localhost:8083`).
2. Run **Auth > Login** (or **Register** then Login). The login/refresh requests have test scripts that automatically store `accessToken` and `sid` into collection variables.
3. Subsequent requests (Session, Engine proxy) use `{{accessToken}}` as the Bearer automatically.

## Dev mode
When the service runs with `LUKE_AUTH_DEV_MODE=true`, `GET /dev/token?user=` mints an act-as token directly and `GET /session` / the proxy accept an `X-Dev-User` header instead of a WorkOS Bearer. Never enable in shared/prod environments.

item.name: Auth
item.description: The consumer front door. luke-auth — not the browser — owns the conversation with WorkOS.

request.description:
Create the WorkOS identity (and optionally provision into core-engine).

**Body**: `email`, `password`, `firstName`, `lastName` (email + password required).

**201** → `{ userId, workosUserId, verifyRequired }`.
**400** if email/password missing; **502** if engine provisioning fails.

header.description: Optional — active tenant/org for the session view.

request.description:
Email/password login against WorkOS.

**200** → `{ accessToken, sid, session }`. The refresh token is set as the `wos_refresh` HttpOnly cookie (used by `/auth/refresh`).

The optional `X-Tenant-Id` header selects the active tenant for the embedded session view.

The test script stores `accessToken` and `sid` into collection variables on success.

query.description: google | microsoft | github | apple | authkit (or a raw WorkOS provider value).
Optional opaque state echoed back to the callback.

request.description: **302** redirect to WorkOS for social/SSO login. Open in a browser — the browser is sent to WorkOS and then back to `/auth/callback`. Postman will follow the redirect; disable redirect-following to inspect the `Location` header.

query.description: Authorization code WorkOS appends when redirecting back.
Set by WorkOS instead of code on failure.

request.description:
Finishes the social/SSO redirect. WorkOS calls this with `?code=...`; the gateway exchanges it, sets the `wos_refresh` cookie, and **302**-redirects the browser to the UI callback URL with `#access_token=...&sid=...` in the fragment.

Normally hit by the browser, not Postman — included for reference.

header.description: Optional — active tenant for the refreshed session view.

request.description:
Rotate the access token from the `wos_refresh` HttpOnly cookie (set by login/callback). Postman sends the cookie automatically if it was captured on the same `baseUrl`.

**200** → `{ accessToken, sid, session }`; **401** if the cookie is missing/expired (the cookie is then cleared).

The test script refreshes the stored `accessToken`/`sid`.

header.description: Optional — if present, the matching WorkOS session is also ended.

request.description:
Clears the `wos_refresh` cookie. If a valid Bearer is supplied, also returns `workosLogoutUrl` to end the WorkOS session.

**200** → `{ ok: true, workosLogoutUrl? }`.

header.description: Optional — active tenant/org; validated against the user's memberships.
Dev-mode only: literal engine userId, stands in for a WorkOS Bearer.

request.description:
Who the user is and what they can do. Verifies the WorkOS access token, derives the engine userId, and merges the core-engine (Camunda) + capability-engine views.

**200** → `{ userId, provisioned, operator, tenantAdmin, tenant, tenants[], roles{}, candidateGroups[], capabilities{}, can[] }`.
**401** if the Bearer is missing/invalid; **403** if `X-Tenant-Id` is a tenant the user doesn't belong to.

A not-yet-onboarded user returns `provisioned:false` with empty roles/tenants (still 200).

exec: console.log('Stored service token as accessToken for downstream calls');

request.description:
Robot equivalent of login. With a valid `X-Service-Key`, mints a short-lived act-as token for the key's service userId.

**200** → `{ token, tokenType: "Bearer", expiresIn, subject }`; **401** for unknown/missing key.

The test script stores the minted token into `accessToken` so you can drive the Engine proxy as the service account.

item.name: Dev tools
item.description: Available only when the service runs with luke.auth.dev-mode=true. Return 404 otherwise.

query.description: Literal engine userId to mint an act-as token for.

request.description:
Dev-only: mints an act-as token for a given engine userId so downstream resource servers can be exercised locally without a real login.

**200** → `{ token }` (only when `luke.auth.dev-mode=true`); **404** otherwise.

NEVER enable dev-mode in shared/prod — it hands out act-as-anyone tokens.

Publishes the gateway's public signing key as a JWK Set. `luke-core-engine` fetches this to verify the act-as-user tokens. Intentionally unauthenticated; only the public half of the keypair is exposed.
Spring Boot actuator health check (used by Render).

item.name: Engine proxy
item.description: Catch-all reverse proxy: any path not claimed by a specific mapping above is forwarded to luke-core-engine, swapping the WorkOS Bearer for a minted act-as-user token. Method, path, query, body, and X-Tenant-Id are forwarded unchanged.

header.description: Optional — forwarded to the engine unchanged.
Dev-mode only: stands in for a WorkOS Bearer.

request.description:
Replace the path with any luke-core-engine API path. The gateway authenticates you (WorkOS Bearer or, in dev mode, `X-Dev-User`), mints an act-as-user token, and forwards the request to the engine transparently.

**401** if authentication fails; **502** if the engine is unreachable; otherwise relays the engine's status, headers, and body.
